description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly white surface, accented by patches of reddish-brown and subtle gray streaks. The rock's surface is rough and uneven, with visible cracks, crevices, and natural weathering patterns. One side features a prominent reddish-brown mineral deposit, suggesting iron oxide staining, while the rest of the rock is mostly a chalky white. The edges are jagged and angular, with some smoother, worn areas indicating exposure to the elements. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings. Its overall form suggests it could serve as a natural barrier or decorative element in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A rough, white rock with reddish-brown patches and jagged, weathered surfaces, featuring natural cracks and mineral stains.
description_view_0: This view highlights the rock's broad, flat face with a large reddish-brown patch and visible surface texture.
description_view_1: This angle shows a more tapered end of the rock, with fewer color variations and a smoother, sloped surface.
description_view_2: The rock appears elongated from this side, with subtle gray streaks and less prominent reddish-brown coloration.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the angular end with a concentrated reddish-brown mineral deposit and sharp edges.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
